text.title: Unit and Integration Testing Patterns
text.summary: Unit tests with mockall trait abstractions and integration tests using tower::ServiceExt::oneshot.

rule.statement: Define a DatabaseTrait (or Repository trait) abstraction and mock it with mockall::mock!. Never mock a concrete struct whose methods use sqlx::query! macros — those embed file paths at compile time.
rule.rationale: sqlx::query! macros require DATABASE_URL at compile time. Mocking the concrete Database struct breaks when these macros are present. A trait abstraction separates the DB interface from the implementation.

section.title: Integration Tests with oneshot

rule.statement: Use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot per request in integration tests. The oneshot call consumes the router, so create a fresh app per test case.
rule.rationale: Agents attempt to send a second request to the same router instance after oneshot and get a cryptic error. oneshot is not repeatable; construct a new router or clone before each call.

section.note: setup_app() must use testcontainers or a transactional rollback wrapper for DB isolation. Never leave it as todo!().
